message:
fix(doctor): escape glob special characters in archive paths

Applies the same fix from parser.rs to doctor.rs for consistency:

Previously, archive paths containing glob special characters like [, ],
or * would cause health check pattern matching to fail or match unintended
files. For example, an archive path like "/data/test[1]/archive" would
have its required file checks fail incorrectly.

This fix:
- Splits the pattern into directory and filename components
- Escapes the directory portion using glob::Pattern::escape()
- Reconstructs the full pattern with only the filename portion as a glob

This ensures that archive paths with special characters (common in
auto-generated directories or paths with version numbers like "project[v2]")
are handled correctly during health checks.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
